content.body: FYI, regarding [html#11933](https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11933) (importing text modules), I intend to ask for Stage 3 for the corresponding [TC39 proposal](https://github.com/tc39/proposal-import-text) at its upcoming meeting next week. I also hope that with its [PR #10](https://github.com/tc39/proposal-import-text/pull/10) we'll be able to resolve the remaining concerns about the handling of the import of non-text files as text. Merging that PR should not require any changes to the HTML spec PR.
content.formatted_body: FYI, regarding html#11933 (importing text modules), I intend to ask for Stage 3 for the corresponding TC39 proposal at its upcoming meeting next week. Merging that PR should not require any changes to the HTML spec PR.

content.body:
Howdy. I've been reading 4.9.12.2 of the HTML table specifications (https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/tables.html#header-and-data-cell-semantics) and I was hoping to get some clarification on how the relationships between data cells and headers are formed. 

I'm focusing on step 10 and I'm trying to understand what would happen if a table used both `headers` on `<td>`, and `scope` on `<th>`. Would the `<td>` have the `<th>` with `scope` added to its headers list? Or would it be ignored? Or would it be replaced? 

can `scope` override `headers`?
content.formatted_body:
